audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's response to Bildad: impossibility of litigating with God, cosmic power, legal imagery, innocence under overwhelming force, and longing for a mediator/umpire.

verses.restored: Job answered and said:
Truly I know it is so; but how can a mortal be righteous with the Divine?
If one desired to contend with him, he could not answer him one time out of a thousand.
Wise in heart and strong in power, who has hardened himself against him and been whole?
He removes mountains, and they do not know it, when he overturns them in his anger.
He shakes the earth from its place, and its pillars tremble.
He commands the sun, and it does not rise; he seals up the stars.
He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.
He makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.
He does great things beyond searching, and wonders without number.
Look, he passes by me, and I do not see; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.
Look, he snatches away; who can turn him back? Who can say to him, What are you doing?
The Divine will not turn back his anger; beneath him bowed the helpers of Rahab.
How then can I answer him, choosing my words with him?
Though I were righteous, I could not answer; I would plead for favor from my judge.
If I called and he answered me, I would not believe that he was listening to my voice.
For he crushes me with a storm and multiplies my wounds for nothing.
He will not let me take my breath, but fills me with bitterness.
If it is a matter of strength, look, he is mighty; if of justice, who will summon him?
Though I were righteous, my own mouth would condemn me; though I were whole, it would declare me twisted.
I am whole; I do not know my soul. I reject my life.
It is all one; therefore I say, He brings the whole and the wicked to an end.
When a scourge kills suddenly, he laughs at the testing of the innocent.
The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges. If not he, then who?
My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away; they see no good.
They pass by like reed boats, like an eagle swooping on prey.
If I say, I will forget my complaint, leave my face, and brighten up,
I become afraid of all my pains; I know you will not hold me innocent.
I am declared wicked; why then do I labor in vain?
If I wash myself with snow water and cleanse my hands with lye,
then you would plunge me into the pit, and my own garments would abhor me.
For he is not a man like me, that I might answer him, that we might come together in judgment.
There is no mediator between us, who might lay his hand on us both.
Let him remove his rod from me, and let his terror not frighten me.
Then I would speak and not fear him, for I am not so within myself.
